user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Alpine Ski House.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 11:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 10:54 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 10:36 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-02-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:16:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:23:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-D trap (Invents attendee role when context is absent); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-D failure mode (Invents attendee role when context is absent)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-D -- Invents attendee role when context is absent
